Forum experience proposes semantic polarity pairing as an idea-generation path. This placeholder preserves only role names and requires human semantic review, registry validation and correlation prescreening.
Forum experience describes industry-relative ranking and temporal normalization as a study family. The public candidate contains no field, submitted expression, result or execution code.

Forum experience proposes a two-role taxonomy for hypothesis generation. This non-executable public intake record preserves only generic roles and requires private registry, semantic, and correlation validation before any formula is constructed.

Forum experience describes pairing two economic information roles only after recording their expected polarity and relationship hypothesis. This public record retains no field, formula, result, or correlation claim and requires private registry, semantic, scope, and correlation validation.

Forum experience suggests compressing a current private registry into a human-reviewed semantic taxonomy before research planning. This public record retains no field names, patterns, prompts, formula, or result.

Forum experience reports that template and AI-generated candidates can reference unavailable operators or nonexistent fields. This non-executable public intake record preserves only the profile-scoped compilation contract and requires current registry and human semantic validation before any private formula is constructed.

Forum experience recommends field-shape, coverage, cadence, distribution, and semantic review before research ideation. This non-executable public intake record retains only generic role descriptors and requires current private registry and human validation before any candidate exists.

Forum template posts support decomposing a broad idea into role and scope axes before any private field or formula is selected. This record is non-executable and contains no source expression or datafield.

Forum PPA discussions support treating a theme as a bounded research brief with signal role, neutralization role, diversity question, validation plan, and stop condition. No reward, eligibility, or submission rule is inferred.

Community research notes support starting from a falsifiable economic question, then selecting data and bounded validation questions. This is a non-executable research brief, not a platform rule or formula template.
Regional and correlation-reduction posts support recording scope and counterexamples before testing a diversity hypothesis. No region, operator, threshold, or correlation outcome is guaranteed.
